tweets.title:
Defence Research & Development Laboratory DRDL of DRDO on Saturday achieved a path-breaking milestone in the development of hypersonic missiles by successfully conducting an extensive long-duration test of its actively cooled full scale scramjet combustor.

A run-time of over 1,200 seconds was achieved at the state-of-the-art scramjet connect pipe test (SCPT) facility in Hyderabad, building upon the earlier successful test of over 700 seconds conducted in January this year.

This successful test positions India at the forefront of advanced aerospace capabilities and continuously emerging war technologies. The remarkable feat is achieved through cutting-edge supersonic air-breathing engine, which uses indigenously developed liquid hydrocarbon endothermic fuel, high temperature thermal barrier coating and advanced manufacturing processes. 

The ground-tests conducted at the SCPT facility have successfully validated the design of advanced active cooled scramjet combustor as well as the capabilities of  test facility.

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
9th May 2026

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.

● The US-Iran ceasefire continues to hold as Qatar throws its support behind Pakistan-led mediation efforts aimed at reaching a lasting diplomatic agreement and reducing regional tensions.

● The IDF dismantled an Islamic Jihad weapons production site and weapons storage facility in northern Gaza. The site was also used by Hamas for manufacturing explosive devices and storing weapons targeting Israeli civilians and IDF troops.

● The Qatar-owned LNG carrier Al Kharaitiyat was approaching the Strait of Hormuz en route to Pakistan’s Port Qasim.

● Bahrain’s Ministry of Interior announced the arrest of 41 individuals linked to the IRGC, describing it as a successful operation against foreign-backed security threats.

● Trump warned that if negotiations with Iran fail to reach a conclusion, the US will resume and expand its naval escort mission, reinstating Project Freedom as “Project Freedom Plus”.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 58 commercial vessels and disabled 4 vessels since April 13 to prevent them from entering or leaving Iranian ports in violation of the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF.

tweets.title:
Who is India's next CDS, Lt. Gen. NS Raja Subramani?

Commissioned into the Garhwal Rifles in December 1985, Lt Gen Subramani has a career spanning over 37 years in the Indian Army. 
Over the course of his service, he has served across a wide range of conflict zones and terrains. He has held several Command, Staff and Instructional appointments.

Lt Gen Subramani is a graduate of the National Defence Academy (NDA) and the Indian Military Academy (IMA).
He has also attended the Joint Services Command and Staff College in Bracknell, United Kingdom, and the National Defence College in New Delhi.

He also holds a Master of Arts degree from King's College London and an MPhil in Defence Studies from Madras University. 

He has been awarded some of India’s highest military honours:
Param Vishisht Seva Medal (PVSM)
Ati Vishisht Seva Medal (AVSM)
Sena Medal
Vishisht Seva Medal

Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani is currently serving as the Military Adviser at the National Security Council Secretariat, a position he has held since September 1, 2025.
Before this, he served as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff from July 1, 2024, to July 31, 2025.

tweets.title:
China Confirms On‑Ground Role In Supporting Pakistan During Operation Sindoor

China has, for the first time, officially admitted that its personnel were present on Pakistani soil during Operation Sindoor, providing direct technical support to the Pakistan Air Force.

This revelation came through China’s state broadcaster CCTV, where engineers from the Aviation Industry Corporation of China (AVIC) described their involvement in supporting Pakistani operations. 

Zhang Heng, an engineer from AVIC’s Chengdu Aircraft Design and Research Institute, recounted his experiences at Pakistani air bases during the conflict. He described the constant roar of fighter jets, the wail of air‑raid sirens, and the extreme heat that reached 50 degrees Celsius by late morning.

The admission marks a significant moment in the India‑Pakistan conflict narrative, as Beijing had previously refrained from acknowledging any on‑ground role in the confrontation.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Day 3 Outcome

Day 3 witnessed intense drone duels and the expansion of the conflict into the maritime domain as India enforced multi-domain pressure on Pakistan.
1. Air Defence Mastery: Indian forces achieved near-total interception of over 600 Pakistani drones. The IACCS system proved highly effective, integrating multiple sensors and platforms to maintain a robust air defence shield along the IB and LoC.
2. Naval Compellence: The forward deployment of the INS Vikrant Carrier Battle Group into the northern Arabian Sea created a powerful deterrent. By positioning strike assets within range of Karachi Port, India imposed serious economic and strategic pressure on Pakistan’s primary maritime lifeline.
3. Strategic Signalling: India demonstrated its ability to operate simultaneously across land, air, and sea domains. The naval posturing added a new layer of compellence, pinning Pakistani naval forces and limiting their options for escalation.
4. Diplomatic Resolve: PM Modi’s firm response to the US Vice President sent a clear message — India will not be deterred and remains prepared to escalate on its own terms if Pakistan continues its aggression.
